# Implementation Plan: Compaction Detection and Recovery

**Branch**: `003-compaction-recovery` | **Date**: 2026-03-08 | **Spec**: [spec.md](spec.md)
**Input**: Feature specification from `/specs/003-compaction-recovery/spec.md`

**Note**: This plan was generated by the `/speckit.plan` command.

## Summary

Implement a hook-based system that detects auto-compaction in Claude Code
sessions, automatically invokes the existing compaction-audit to identify
tainted edits, orchestrates reverts, triggers /clear, and resumes the
interrupted task with full context. The system uses PreCompact and
SessionStart hooks to capture state and inject recovery instructions,
with tmux automation for zero-manual-step recovery.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: Bash 5.x (POSIX-compatible subset for portability)
**Primary Dependencies**: jq (JSON parsing), git (file reverts), tmux (optional automation)
**Storage**: Filesystem — JSON markers, markdown logs, temp files in `.claude/`
**Testing**: Health-check command (FR-075), dry-run mode (FR-051), manual simulation
**Target Platform**: macOS (Apple Silicon/Intel) + WSL2 development
**Project Type**: CLI scripts + hook configuration (development infrastructure)
**Performance Goals**: Hook script <30s (SC-014), full recovery <2min typical
**Constraints**: 2KB preamble limit (FR-083), 0600 permissions (FR-091), post-compaction context budget
**Scale/Scope**: Single developer, single machine, <20 tainted edits typical case

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.*

| # | Principle | Relevance | Status |
|---|-----------|-----------|--------|
| I | Documentation-Is-the-Product | Protects doc workflow from compaction damage | PASS |
| II | Threat-Model Driven (NON-NEGOTIABLE) | Threat: tainted context produces incorrect hardening guidance | PASS |
| III | Free-First | jq, git, tmux, bash — all free/OSS | PASS |
| IV | Cite Canonical Sources | Claude Code hooks API, git documentation | PASS |
| V | Every Recommendation Is Verifiable | Health-check (FR-075), dry-run (FR-051) | PASS |
| VI | Bash Scripts Are Infrastructure | All scripts: `set -euo pipefail`, shellcheck, idempotent | MUST ENFORCE |
| VII | Defense in Depth | Detect → Audit → Revert → Resume (4 layers) | PASS |
| VIII | Explicit Over Clever | Clear error messages, step-by-step status output | MUST ENFORCE |
| IX | Markdown Quality Gate | Recovery logs are operational, not documentation | N/A |
| X | CLI-First Infrastructure | All components are CLI scripts and hook configs | PASS |

**Gate result**: PASS — no violations. Principles VI and VIII require active
enforcement during implementation (shellcheck CI, colored output, quoted variables).

## Project Structure

### Documentation (this feature)

```text
specs/003-compaction-recovery/
├── plan.md              # This file
├── research.md          # Phase 0: research findings
├── data-model.md        # Phase 1: entity definitions
├── quickstart.md        # Phase 1: setup guide
├── contracts/           # Phase 1: interface contracts
│   ├── hook-compact.md  # SessionStart(compact) contract
│   ├── hook-clear.md    # SessionStart(clear) contract
│   ├── hook-precompact.md  # PreCompact contract
│   ├── recovery-log.md  # Log format specification
│   └── batch-mode.md    # compaction-audit batch extension
└── tasks.md             # Phase 2: generated by /speckit.tasks
```

### Source Code (repository root + dotfiles)

```text
~/dotfiles/scripts/bin/          # New scripts (symlinked to ~/bin/)
├── recovery-detect.sh           # SessionStart(compact) hook handler
├── recovery-precompact.sh       # PreCompact hook handler
├── recovery-loader.sh           # SessionStart(clear) context injector
├── recovery-watcher.sh          # Background /clear trigger (tmux only)
└── recovery-health.sh           # Infrastructure health-check

~/dotfiles/claude/.claude/
└── settings.json                # Hook definitions (updated)

.claude/commands/
└── compaction-audit.md          # Extended with --batch support

.claude/                         # Runtime artifacts (not version-controlled)
├── recovery-marker.json         # State marker (transient)
├── recovery-interrupted-task.json  # Captured task (transient)
└── recovery-logs/               # Persistent recovery logs
    └── recovery-<timestamp>.md
```

**Structure Decision**: Scripts + hook config infrastructure pattern.
Scripts live in `~/dotfiles/scripts/bin/` per project convention (FR-013)
and are symlinked to `~/bin/`. Runtime artifacts live in the project
`.claude/` directory. Recovery logs persist across /clear for post-hoc review.

## Architecture Overview

### Hook Flow

```text
                    ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
                    │           Claude Code Session                │
                    │                                             │
  Context hits  ───►│  PreCompact hook fires                      │
  compaction        │    ├── Write recovery marker (stage: detected)│
  threshold         │    ├── Capture interrupted task from transcript│
                    │    └── Update marker (stage: task_captured)   │
                    │                                             │
  Auto-compaction ──►│  SessionStart(compact) hook fires           │
  occurs            │    ├── Read/verify recovery marker           │
                    │    ├── Inject additionalContext:             │
                    │    │   "HALT. Run /compaction-audit --batch" │
                    │    └── In tmux: start recovery-watcher.sh    │
                    │                                             │
  Model responds ──►│  Model runs /compaction-audit --batch        │
                    │    ├── Identifies tainted edits              │
                    │    ├── Auto-reverts without confirmation     │
                    │    └── Writes audit-complete sentinel        │
                    │                                             │
  Watcher detects ─►│  recovery-watcher.sh (tmux only)            │
  sentinel          │    ├── Wait for model idle (prompt visible)  │
                    │    ├── Send /clear via tmux send-keys        │
                    │    └── Clean up sentinel                    │
                    │                                             │
  /clear fires ────►│  SessionStart(clear) hook fires              │
                    │    ├── Check for recovery marker             │
                    │    ├── If present: load interrupted task     │
                    │    ├── Load CARRYOVER (feature 002 integration)│
                    │    ├── Inject combined context via           │
                    │    │   additionalContext                     │
                    │    ├── Consume (delete) recovery marker      │
                    │    └── Clean up temp files                   │
                    │                                             │
  Fresh session ───►│  Model resumes interrupted task              │
                    │    ├── Confirms understanding                │
                    │    ├── Verifies file state                  │
                    │    └── Continues work                       │
                    └─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Degradation Tiers (FR-093)

| Tier | Missing Component | Capability |
|------|-------------------|------------|
| 1 | (none) | Full recovery: reverts + CARRYOVER + task + log + auto-/clear |
| 2 | No CARRYOVER | Reverts + interrupted task + log + auto-/clear |
| 3 | No transcript | Reverts + CARRYOVER + log + auto-/clear (no task capture) |
| 4 | No git | CARRYOVER + interrupted task + log + auto-/clear (no reverts) |
| 5 | No filesystem writes | additionalContext injection only (detection + context) |
| 6 | Minimal | Compaction detected, developer warned via context injection |

### Script Responsibilities

| Script | Hook Event | Trigger | Key FRs |
|--------|-----------|---------|---------|
| `recovery-precompact.sh` | PreCompact | Auto-compaction pending | FR-004, FR-010, FR-023, FR-062 |
| `recovery-detect.sh` | SessionStart | `compact` source | FR-001, FR-002, FR-011, FR-016, FR-018 |
| `recovery-loader.sh` | SessionStart | `clear` source | FR-008, FR-009, FR-029, FR-044, FR-045 |
| `recovery-watcher.sh` | (background) | Spawned by detect | FR-006, FR-007, FR-020 |
| `recovery-health.sh` | (manual) | User invocation | FR-075 |

## Complexity Tracking

No constitution violations to justify.
